test_cases.name: [SynchronizedBeforeSuite]

[It] Running the Cluster API E2E tests Should successfully scale out and scale in a MachineDeployment Should successfully scale a MachineDeployment up and down upon changes to the MachineDeployment replica count

test_cases.failure_message:
Timed out after 1200.001s.
KubeadmControlPlane object k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-3niv1k/k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-6uaq0k-control-plane was not initialized in time
The function passed to Eventually failed at /home/prow/go/src/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api-provider-azure/test/e2e/common.go:265 with:
KubeadmControlPlane is not yet initialized
Expected
    <bool>: false
to be true
test_cases.failure_body:
[FAILED] Timed out after 1200.001s.
KubeadmControlPlane object k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-3niv1k/k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-6uaq0k-control-plane was not initialized in time
The function passed to Eventually failed at /home/prow/go/src/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api-provider-azure/test/e2e/common.go:265 with:
KubeadmControlPlane is not yet initialized
Expected
    <bool>: false
to be true
In [It] at: /home/prow/go/src/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api-provider-azure/test/e2e/common.go:266 @ 06/09/26 21:39:19.528

ai_summary.summary: KubeadmControlPlane failed to initialize due to Azure VM allocation failure in a specific zone

ai_analysis.root_cause: The KubeadmControlPlane object k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-6uaq0k-control-plane failed to initialize because its first control plane machine (k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-6uaq0k-control-plane-q5z6q) could not be provisioned. The AzureMachine YAML at artifacts/clusters/bootstrap/resources/k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-3niv1k/AzureMachine/k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-6uaq0k-control-plane-q5z6q.yaml shows the VM was configured with vmSize: Standard_B2s and was placed in zone 3 (visible in the activity log's disk resource tags). The azure-activity-logs at artifacts/clusters/k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-6uaq0k/azure-activity-logs/capz-e2e-lfxd84.log contains a 'ZonalAllocationFailed' error (line 296) with the message: 'Allocation failed. We do not have sufficient capacity for the requested VM size in this zone.' This infrastructure provisioning failure prevented the machine from becoming ready, which in turn prevented the KubeadmControlPlane from initializing. The CAPZ controller logs also show repeated 'ZonalAllocationFailed' errors when trying to create the Azure VM.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Modify the cluster template to either use a different VM size that has capacity in zone 3 of the CanadaCentral region, or specify a different availability zone for the control plane machines. Alternatively, implement a retry mechanism in the provisioning logic to attempt allocation in different zones when ZonalAllocationFailed errors occur. The specific error is in artifacts/clusters/k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-6uaq0k/azure-activity-logs/capz-e2e-lfxd84.log at line 296 where it shows 'Allocation failed. We do not have sufficient capacity for the requested VM size in this zone.'

test_cases.failure_message:
Timed out after 1200.000s.
Timed out waiting for 3 control plane machines to exist
Expected
    <int>: 2
to equal
    <int>: 3
test_cases.failure_body:
[FAILED] Timed out after 1200.000s.
Timed out waiting for 3 control plane machines to exist
Expected
    <int>: 2
to equal
    <int>: 3
In [It] at: /home/prow/go/pkg/mod/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api/test@v1.13.2/framework/controlplane_helpers.go:115 @ 06/09/26 21:44:37.181

ai_summary.summary: Control plane machine failed to provision due to Azure capacity issues

ai_analysis.root_cause: The test failure occurred because only 2 out of 3 control plane machines were successfully provisioned within the timeout period. Investigation revealed that the third control plane machine (k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-cirl41-control-plane-779jf) failed during Azure VM provisioning due to a 'ZonalAllocationFailed' error, as shown in the CAPZ controller logs. The error message from artifacts/clusters/bootstrap/logs/capz-system/capz-controller-manager/capz-controller-manager-d6c54db8f-md775/manager.log indicates: 'Allocation failed. We do not have sufficient capacity for the requested VM size in this zone.' The AzureMachine resource shows vmState: Failed, confirming the provisioning failure. This prevented the control plane from reaching the expected 3-machine configuration, causing the test to timeout waiting for all control plane machines to exist.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Modify the test to use a different Azure region or VM size that has sufficient capacity, or implement retry logic with fallback options when encountering zonal allocation failures. Alternatively, consider using availability sets instead of zones for control plane machines in test environments where capacity issues are common. The specific file to modify would be the test configuration that specifies the Azure region and VM size for control plane machines.
